issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: Revenue is calculated without filtering for completed transactions only.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS revenue

issues.title: Missing filter for transaction status
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status, which is required for correct revenue calculation.

issues.title: Unnecessary columns in GROUP BY
issues.problem: Including m.email in the GROUP BY clause is unnecessary and can be removed.

issues.title: Missing comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each clause and calculation.

issues.title: PII exposure
issues.problem: Exposing email addresses in the result set can lead to privacy risks.
issues.fix: Remove m.email from the SELECT clause.

issues.title: Potential performance issue with implicit join
issues.line: JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
issues.problem: The query uses an implicit join which can be less efficient than an explicit JOIN.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT t.merchant_id, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS revenue, m.merchant_name FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01' AND t.status IN ('COMPLETED', 'FAILED') GROUP BY t.merchant_id, m.merchant_name;
review.summary: The query had critical correctness issues with revenue calculation and missing status filter. It also exposed PII and lacked readability and performance optimizations.

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query calculates revenue without filtering for completed transactions only.
issues.fix: SELECT SUM(amount) AS revenue FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions WHERE status = 'COMPLETED'

issues.title: Potential performance issue with implicit join
issues.problem: Using merchant_id without a JOIN clause can lead to implicit join behavior, which can be inefficient.
issues.fix: SELECT t.merchant_id, m.merchant_name FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments on complex logic
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions) AS failure_rate
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the calculation of the failure rate.
issues.fix: /* Calculate failure rate as the percentage of failed transactions */ SELECT COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions) AS failure_rate FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions WHERE status = 'FAILED'

review.corrected_sql: WITH completed_transactions AS (SELECT SUM(amount) AS revenue FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions WHERE status = 'COMPLETED'), total_transactions AS (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions), failed_transactions AS (SELECT COUNT(*) AS failed FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions WHERE status = 'FAILED') SELECT revenue, (failed * 100.0 / total) AS failure_rate FROM completed_transactions, total_transactions, failed_transactions; /* Calculate revenue and failure rate */
review.summary: The query had issues with revenue calculation, potential performance issues, and readability. Corrected the revenue calculation, added comments for readability, and ensured joins are explicit.

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status 'COMPLETED' to calculate revenue.

issues.title: Missing join with dim_merchant
issues.problem: The query should join with dim_merchant to get merchant names instead of showing merchant IDs.

issues.title: Potential performance issue with correlated subquery
issues.problem: Using correlated subqueries can lead to performance issues. It's better to use a single query with conditional aggregation.
issues.fix: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'COMPLETED' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS revenue, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) * 100 AS failure_rate FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which makes it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'COMPLETED' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS revenue, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) * 100 AS failure_rate FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant ON sigma_analytics.fact_transactions.merchant_id = sigma_analytics.dim_merchant.merchant_id
review.summary: The query had issues with revenue calculation, missing join with dim_merchant, potential performance issues, and readability.

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate calculation is incorrect as it uses COUNT(FAILED) which will always be zero because 'FAILED' is a string, not a boolean.

issues.title: Potential performance issue with implicit join
issues.problem: The query uses implicit joins which can lead to Cartesian products and performance issues.

issues.title: Lack of comments on complex logic
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, making it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining each part of the query

issues.title: Inconsistent naming convention
issues.problem: The naming convention is inconsistent (e.g., 'FAILED' vs 'COMPLETED' casing).

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'COMPLETED' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS revenue,
    (COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS failure_rate
FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions ft
JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant dm ON ft.merchant_id = dm.merchant_id
WHERE ft.status IN ('COMPLETED', 'FAILED');
review.summary: The query has critical correctness issues with failure rate calculation and potential performance issues with implicit joins. Readability can be improved with comments and consistent naming.
